meanings.definition: British nuclear physicist (born in Germany) honored for his contributions to quantum mechanics (1882-1970)
put up with something or somebody unpleasant
examples: I cannot bear his constant criticism
The new secretary had to endure a lot of unprofessional remarks
he learned to tolerate the heat
She stuck out two years in a miserable marriage

meanings.definition: move while holding up or supporting

examples: The apple tree bore delicious apples this year
The unidentified plant bore gorgeous flowers

meanings.definition: take on as one's own the expenses or debts of another person
examples: She agreed to bear the responsibility
How much does this savings certificate pay annually?
They conducted themselves well during these difficult times

meanings.definition: have rightfully; of rights, titles, and offices
examples: She bears the title of Duchess
He held the governorship for almost a decade

meanings.definition: support or hold in a certain manner

synonyms: have a bun in the oven
examples: The are expecting another child in January
he was a child born of adultery
